behavior_summary: When the player 'looks at' (examines) this item, the script builds a short human-readable description and shows it as a single bark/description line.

The routine reads the item instance's frame/type (to select a noun phrase) and the stack quantity (to prefix a number and choose singular vs plural wording), concatenates those pieces into a string, and displays that string to the player.

Different ranges of frame codes map to different item-type names (e.g., vial(s) of blood, pile(s) of bone shards, pile(s) of wood, executioner's hood(s), etc.); the code then applies quantity-based pluralization.
The final constructed string is presented via a Bark node (short UI text) and the U8P_ERTHREAG::look process then suspends/ends.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ERTHREAG::look begins by obtaining the implicit item context (this) then calls Item::getFrame and Item::getQuantity to populate locals used for branching.
A sequence of IfStatement branches tests the frame code value to select one of several noun phrases; within those branches the code also branches on quantity to apply singular vs plural suffixes.
The description string is built incrementally (numToStr(quantity) as prefix, then noun phrase and optional pluralization) and only one Bark node is emitted with the composed text.
No global flags are read or written by this U8P_ERTHREAG::look process; control flow is local to the function and driven by the frame/quantity values.
open_questions: Exact numeric ranges for some frame-code to name mappings are inferred from strings in the dump; can the original mapping table be confirmed to ensure no off-by-one ranges?

The extracted bark sample shows duplicated or concatenated fragments (likely decompiler noise). Is the intended phrasing for each branch available from original resources to verify spacing/punctuation?
Are there any additional edge cases (zero quantity, extremely large stacks, or special frame codes) that should be handled but are not visible in this excerpt?

main_function.name: U8P_ERTHREAG::look
main_function.purpose: Produce and display a short item description when the player examines the object: prefix the quantity, append an item-type noun determined by the item's frame, and apply singular/plural wording as appropriate.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the U8P_ERTHREAG::look/examine action on the item (the object's U8P_ERTHREAG::look handler is executed).

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHREAG::look
function_map.role: Builds a quantity-prefixed, frame-code-dependent short description and displays it to the player (Bark).

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Read the item's frame/type code to select the appropriate noun/phrase.

function_map.name: Item::getQuantity
function_map.role: Return the item's stack quantity used for numeric prefixing and pluralization decisions.

function_map.name: numToStr
function_map.role: Convert a numeric quantity to a string for concatenation in the description.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the item's frame/type code used to select the noun phrase describing the item.
Obtain the item stack quantity to prefix the description and choose singular vs plural wording.
Convert the numeric quantity into text before concatenating with the noun phrase.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the player 'looks at' (examines) this item, the script builds a short human-readable description and shows it as a single bark/description line.
